summary: Round 8 correctly resolves both round-7 objections. The T26↔T31 circular dependency is eliminated: T31.depends_on no longer includes T26, and the closure modal Dialog and confirmation banner are now inline components within JointChatView (T26) with a clear decision note explaining the architectural choice. The T21 dependency direction error is fully fixed: T21.depends_on is now ['T11','T17','T20'] (T19 removed), and T19, T24, and T26 all correctly list T21 in their depends_on, establishing the acyclic chain T21→consumers. Coverage, fanout, NFR library, and NFR application are all solid — all 15 user stories have wired tickets, all 6 NFRs are in the library, and NFR omissions examined (T6 lacking NFR-privacy, T30 lacking NFR-privacy) are both correctly justified (T6 is a server-side utility whose callers carry the privacy NFR; T30 only reads phase-level boolean closureProposed, not raw private transcript content). Phase 0 tickets each satisfy all three verification criteria: human action specified, secret/config artifact named, and smoke-check verification proven. Sizing is clean — all tickets carry exactly one surface value. The dependency graph is acyclic with no phantom edges identified. Playwright ownership is correctly applied to all user-visible feature tickets; T8 (ChatWindow) is cross-cutting infrastructure whose coverage is exercised transitively through the views that use it. Testing realism is exemplary: convex-test is used for all Convex functions, Vitest for pure utilities, Playwright + CLAUDE_MOCK for E2E, with the Anthropic API correctly treated as an external boundary where mocking is appropriate. Design tokens, loading/error/empty states, and accessibility ACs are consistently woven into every user-visible ticket. Privacy enforcement layers (prompt, filter, DB access control, UI) are all represented in the plan with explicit ACs on the relevant tickets. Decisions are surfaced for every non-obvious inference.
